text: The surgeon carefully dissects to isolate the cystic duct. Once isolated, the cystic duct is clipped three times: two clips are placed proximally on the shoulder of the duct, and one clip is placed distally.
The surgeon prepares to transect or cut the cystic duct, ensuring the operating field is clear by cleaning the blood vessels for better visibility.
The surgeon isolates the cystic artery, applying clips to secure it before transecting the artery to separate it from surrounding tissue.
